title: Smoky German Cheese Casserole
description: A comforting, low‑calorie German‑style cheese bake that glows pink with beet powder and gets a smoky finish on a tandoor grill.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 °C and place a shallow Tandoor pan or grill rack inside for heat retention.
In a medium bowl, whisk together almond milk, Greek yogurt, flour, smoked paprika, pink beet powder, salt and pepper until smooth.
Add the grated Gouda and Emmental to the bowl, stirring until fully incorporated.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. Sauté the diced onion and minced garlic until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Pour the sautéed onion mixture into the cheese blend and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a baking dish or a pre‑heated Tandoor pan, spreading it evenly.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12 minutes until the surface is golden and bubbly.
Remove the dish and carefully place it back on the hot Tandoor grill for 2–3 minutes to develop a smoky crust. Keep a close eye to avoid burning.
Let the casserole rest for 5 minutes, then sprinkle with chopped parsley before serving.
Serve hot as a hearty lunch or dinner.
